rulings.Case Title: LAURA WOODWARD VS. LEGACY PROBATE INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Re-Set Hearing Date For Plaintiff Motion For Relief Purusant To Ccp Section 128.7 Agaunst Defendant Legacy And Its Attorneys John C. Brona And Cornerstone Law Group
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2024, line 3. PLAINTIFF LAURA WOODWARD'S Motion For Relief Purusant To CCP Section 128.7 Against Defendant Legacy and its Attorneys John C. Brona and Cornerstone Law Group. Plaintiffs motion for sanctions is denied. Plaintiff failed to comply with the 21-day safe harbor periods of CCP 128.5(f)(1)(B) or CCP 128.7(c)(1). Plaintiff served the motion on September 20, 2024, and the motion to continue trial was granted on October 8, 2024. (Broadcast Music, Inc. v. Structured Asset Sales, LLC (2022) 75 Cal.App.5th 596, 605 [[I]f the objectionable document is resolved by the court during the safe harbor period, sanctions cannot be awarded.] The court further notes that plaintiff withdrew the motion to deem on September 26, 2024. In any event, this motion substantively fails. Plaintiffs belief that defendants declarations contain inadmissible evidence is a basis for an evidentiary objection, not sanctions. Our adversary system requires that attorneys and litigants be provided substantial breathing room to develop and assert factual and legal arguments. (Peake v. Underwood (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 428, 433.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the courts website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under Online Services, navigate to Tentative Rulings, and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSRs transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHAKESHA NASH-ROBERTSON VS. JASON PETRILLO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2024, line 1. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF CHAKESHA NASH-ROBERTSON'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Appearance required. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: TUCK AIRE HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING CORPORATION VS. MUSIC CITY HOTEL, L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2024, Line 3. PLAINTIFF TUCK AIRE HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING CORPORATION, DOING BUSINESS AS ATLAS HEATING AND VENTILATING COMPANY's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Tuck Aire Heating & Air Conditioning Corporation's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendants shall provide code compliant responses without objections by December 31, 2024. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Defendants did not act with substantial justification. Defendants shall pay to Plaintiff sanctions in the amount of $2,000. The parties are advised that stacking discovery motions is disfavored. Stacked motions may be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice). For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion For Joinder, Declaration, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2024, line 2. PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S Motion For Joinder. Plaintiff Daniel Powells Motion for Joinder is DENIED. The motion is based on CCP 379, which establishes a substantive rule governing when persons may be joined as defendants in a single action. CCP 379, however, is not a vehicle for adding parties as defendants. A plaintiff seeking to add a party as a defendant must follow the procedure for filing an amended complaint. (See CCP 473.) Whether the complaint is sufficient as to any claim or named defendant will turn on a review of the allegations and claims stated in the complaint. Here, Plaintiff has not submitted an amended complaint or proposed amended complaint and, therefore, there is no basis to determine whether the Police Department, Sheriffs Department or any other party may properly be added as a defendant. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the courts website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under Online Services, navigate to Tentative Rulings, and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSRs transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
